page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz and includes a 50 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is limited to a 66 GB temporary disk, and the server is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the D8s_v3 sits primarily in the lower tiers for single-core and memory-bound operations. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is categorized as weak to poor, reflecting the older Intel Broadwell architecture. Multi-core performance is slightly better, achieving average ratings in stress-ng multi-core and PassMark physics tests, though multi-core Geekbench and software compilation tasks remain weak. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks show weak performance, and database operations such as Redis SET and PassMark database tests are similarly constrained. However, the instance maintains average performance for lightweight LLM inference tasks using small 135M models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8s_v3 offers a mature, stable hardware profile with dedicated resources at a lower entry point compared to newer generation instances. The tradeoff lies in its reduced single-threaded compute speed and memory bandwidth. This virtual machine is best suited for general-purpose workloads that require a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio but do not demand high-frequency processing. Appropriate workloads include moderate web serving, development and testing environments, basic multi-threaded application hosting, and lightweight background processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance includes 66 GB of local storage, supports Premium Storage, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor without any integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, though multi-core CPU and lightweight LLM inference tasks achieve average ratings. This hardware profile makes the D8s_v3 a cost-effective option for workloads requiring dedicated resources without demanding high single-threaded performance. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, moderate web serving, and basic multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 66 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor under Hyper-V.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 66 GB of local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and Premium Storage support for stable, everyday workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM providing a balanced general-purpose resource profile
Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 50 MB L3 cache
Includes 66 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations
Delivers average multi-core CPU and lightweight LLM inference performance
Best suited for development environments, moderate web serving, and basic multi-threaded tasks
